mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by >0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of instances where the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.2 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during periods of elevated inflation indicates supply chain bottlenecks and stagflation risks, forcing equity markets to price in lower future cash flows over the next month.
failure_condition: Negative industrial production growth during high inflation periods yields positive 20-day forward returns in more than half of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers immediate expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in >50% of months where CPI inflation exceeds the 0.4% monthly threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, supporting consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising payrolls and falling unemployment fails to produce a positive rank correlation with 60-day forward returns over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth during the early recovery phase signals expanding corporate revenues and improving profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth rates during recovery phases are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of quarterly observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month rebound in industrial production signals rapid manufacturing sector recovery and easing supply constraints, prompting immediate equity market rallies over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of instances where industrial production rises by more than 0.5% month-over-month.
